academy.intro: A considered library of neurology courses — anatomy, diagnostics and device technique — delivered by practising clinicians and engineers.

free.name: Free
free.desc: Knowledge is a shared wealth. Our free tier makes foundational medical education accessible to every clinician — open lectures, core anatomy, and introductory walkthroughs with no barriers.

premium.name: Premium
premium.desc: For clinicians who demand depth. Our premium courses are highly specialized, expert-led clinical deep dives — intensive modules on advanced techniques, rare presentations, and cutting-edge protocols that require dedicated support for our educators.

pastDetail.overview: This session brought together over sixty clinicians from neurology, neurophysiology, emergency medicine, and intensive care for a structured day of expert-led presentations and case-based workshops. The programme centred on the practical management of complex neurological emergencies, with particular attention to the evidence base for current treatment protocols and the clinical situations where that evidence is absent or contested. Speakers were drawn from tertiary referral centres across the region, and the case discussions reflected the genuine diagnostic uncertainty that characterises daily practice at the interface of neurology and critical care. Participants rated the session highly for relevance, and several of the clinical frameworks introduced during the workshops have since been integrated into institutional protocols by attending teams.
pastDetail.infoBody: For access to full session recordings, transcripts or supporting documentation, please write to the atelier — references are shared individually with academy members.

agenda.title: Panel: evidence, uncertainty & protocol
agenda.detail: Where the evidence base is strong, and where clinical judgement must lead.

agenda.title: Case-based breakout sessions
agenda.detail: Worked diagnostic cases drawn from partner referral centres.

agenda.title: Closing discussion & certificates
agenda.detail: Synthesis of the day and issuance of attendance certificates.

faq.a: Yes — full recordings are archived and shared individually with registered academy members on request.

faq.q: Can I obtain a certificate of attendance?
faq.a: Certificates were issued to all registered attendees on the day and can be reissued by writing to the atelier.
The slide decks, reading pack and case material are available in the Documents & Resources section below.
Selected sessions are repeated each year; upcoming dates are published on the Events calendar.

reservation.title: Reserve Your Seat
reservation.intro: Complete the form below to secure your place at this session. A confirmation will be sent to your email shortly after submission.
reservation.eventDesc:
This focused half-day programme brings together neurologists, neurophysiologists, and allied clinical teams for a series of expert-led presentations built around real case material, with time reserved after each talk for questions from the floor.

The morning addresses the acute assessment of the neurologically deteriorating patient — new-onset seizures in the emergency setting, the management of status epilepticus, and a practical framework for evaluating encephalopathy. The afternoon turns to procedural topics: EEG monitoring in the ICU and a workshop-style review of challenging traces.

All registered attendees receive a pre-reading pack, a printed programme on the day, and access to the session slide deck within 72 hours. Certificates of attendance are issued automatically, and seats are limited, so early registration is strongly recommended.
reservation.signInHint: You'll be asked to sign in to confirm your seat.
reservation.modalBody: Your seat for "{title}" is reserved. A confirmation will follow by email.
reservation.success: Your reservation has been received. A confirmation will follow by email.

caseStudies.title: Case Studies & Trade Reports

items.title: 32-Channel EEG Rollout — Hospital Network Modernisation
items.summary: Coordinated import, customs clearance and clinical installation of eighteen digital EEG systems across six partner hospitals, including staff training and a twelve-month service agreement.
items.outcome: Reduced diagnostic backlog by roughly 40% within the first quarter of full deployment, and established a regional service hub for ongoing maintenance.

items.title: Representation Agreement — Vagus Nerve Stimulator Line
items.summary: Secured exclusive regional distribution rights for a non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation platform, including regulatory registration and a tiered clinical training programme for partner neurologists.
items.outcome: Platform now active in eleven clinics across three countries, supported by a dedicated after-sales service network.

items.title: ICU Monitoring Suite — Critical Care Expansion
items.summary: Managed end-to-end import of intracranial pressure monitoring suites for three intensive care units, including cold-chain logistics, customs bonding and clinical handover.
items.outcome: Enabled continuous ICP monitoring capability in two cities for the first time, shortening escalation decision time in critical cases.

items.title: Export Readiness Programme — Compact EMG Devices
items.summary: Guided a manufacturer through market assessment, documentation, certification and freight arrangements for entry into Middle Eastern distribution channels.
items.outcome: Achieved first regional sale within five months of certification, with a standing order now in place for quarterly shipments.

about.pullquote: — a heritage of precision, a future of partnership —

story.title: Built on engineering, drawn by care
story.body: Founded by biomedical engineers with deep clinical experience, Neuro Tech began as a specialised neurology importer and has grown, slowly and deliberately, into a global supplier, academy and agency partner — present in research institutes, hospitals and clinics across four continents.

mission.title: Mission
mission.desc: To render best-in-class neurology equipment and education accessible to clinicians worldwide, through dependable sourcing and uncompromised service.

vision.title: Vision
vision.desc: A global standard of neurological care, supported by considered partnerships between manufacturers, distributors, educators and clinicians.

timeline.event: Founded as a biomedical engineering practice.
Granted national import licence for neurology devices.
First exclusive agency partnership with a European manufacturer.
Expanded into export markets across the Middle East.
Partner network reaches ten countries and two hundred institutions.
Launch of Neuro Tech Academy and the global events programme.

items.title: Precision
items.desc: Every device, document and lesson is held to a clinical standard. Nothing ships, prints or publishes until it is exact.

items.title: Partnership
items.desc: We grow through long, exclusive relationships rather than transactions — with manufacturers, clinicians and educators alike.

items.title: Pedagogy
items.desc: Knowledge is the product. Our academy turns equipment expertise into teachable, examinable clinical skill.

collaborate.title: Collaborate with us
collaborate.desc: Whether you manufacture neurology equipment, lead a clinical department, or teach the next generation of specialists — there is a place for considered partnership here.
